rulings.Case Title: ELVIA JUAREZ VS. ANTHONY E NOVAK et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion To For The Reconsideration Vacate Elvia Juarez' Renewal Of The Judgment, The Memorandum Of Points And Authorities, And The Statements On The Oath
rulings.Rulings: Set for Law and Motion/Discovery Calendar on Friday, May 16, 2025, Line 1. DEFENDANT EDWARD NOVAK's Motion To For The Reconsideration Vacate Elvia Juarez' Renewal Of The Judgment. Continued to May 29, 2025, to be heard in department 301 at 9:30 before Judge Kahn. He is the judge that heard the underlying motion. For the 9:00 a.m. calendar, all attorneys and parties may appear in Department 302 remotely or in person. Remote hearings will be conducted by videoconference using Zoom. (Dept. 302 Zoom ID 160 409 7690; Passcode 516287.) To appear remotely at the hearing, go to the court's website at sfsuperiorcourt.org under "Online Services," navigate to "Tentative Rulings," and click on the appropriate link, or dial the corresponding phone number. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The text of the email shall include the name and contact information, including email address, of the attorney or party who will appear at the hearing. The court no longer provides a court reporter in the Law & Motion Department. Parties may retain their own reporter, who may appear in the courtroom or remotely. A retained reporter must be a California certified court reporter (CSR), for only a CSR's transcript may be used in California courts. If a CSR is being retained, include in your email all of the following: their name, CSR and telephone numbers, and their individual work email address. =(302/JMQ)

rulings.Case Title: JOSEPH SEIDLER ET AL VS. WELLS FARGO CLEARING SERVICES, LLC ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION FOR WITHDRAWAL OF ATTORNEY OF RECORD
rulings.Rulings: Set for Law and Motion/Discovery Calendar on Friday, May 16, 2025, Line 8. APPELLANT JOSEPH SEIDLER's MOTION FOR WITHDRAWAL OF ATTORNEY OF RECORD. Plaintiff counsel Elizabeth A. Sperling and Glaser Weil Fink Howard Jordan Shapiro LLP's unopposed motion to be Relieved as Counsel for Defendant Damion Davis is DENIED WITHOUT PREJUDICE. The trial court loses jurisdiction over most aspects of a case when, as here, an appeal is taken. Moving counsel has not shown acting on the motion to be relieved is within the scope of matters over which this court still has jurisdiction. Moving counsel has filed a motion to be relieved in the Court of Appeal. If that motion is granted, Moving Counsel should file notice of that order in this docket. For the 9:00 a.m. calendar, all attorneys and parties may appear in Department 302 remotely or in person. Remote hearings will be conducted by videoconference using Zoom. (Dept. 302 Zoom ID 160 409 7690; Passcode 516287.) To appear remotely at the hearing, go to the court's website at sfsuperiorcourt.org under "Online Services," navigate to "Tentative Rulings," and click on the appropriate link, or dial the corresponding phone number. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The text of the email shall include the name and contact information, including email address, of the attorney or party who will appear at the hearing. Counsel for the moving party is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the tentative ruling is not contested. The court no longer provides a court reporter in the Law & Motion Department. Parties may retain their own reporter, who may appear in the courtroom or remotely. A retained reporter must be a California certified court reporter (CSR), for only a CSR's transcript may be used in California courts. If a CSR is being retained, include in your email all of the following: their name, CSR and telephone numbers, and their individual work email address. =(302/JMQ)

rulings.Case Title: ROSALIND ARBEL VS. DOMINIC D YIN ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compel Deposition Of Trader Joe'S Employee Ken Amoh
rulings.Rulings: Set for Law and Motion/Discovery Calendar on Friday, May 16, 2025, Line 10. PLAINTIFFs ROSALIND ARBEL,and NIR ARBEL's Motion To Compel Deposition Of Trader Joe'S Employee Ken Amoh. Plaintiffs' motion to compel deposition of Ken Amoh is GRANTED. Plaintiffs Nir and Rosalind Arbel are suing Defendants for an injury Plaintiff Rosalind sustained while walking in a parking lot of a business called Lucky Penny. Plaintiffs are suing Defendants Dominic Yin and Helen Yin as trustees and individually for negligence and premises liability, alleging they owned the premises on which Plaintiff Rosalind tripped. Plaintiffs added Defendant Trader Joe's Company as a defendant in October of 2023. No meet and confer was required here, but Plaintiffs attempted to do so in good faith. Plaintiffs move to compel the deposition of Ken Amoh, an "employee of a party," under CCP Section 2024.450(a) because they failed to object or appear for their noticed deposition. It appears the discovery is reasonably calculated to lead to the discovery of admissible evidence. (CCP Section 2017.010.) Defendant Trader Joe's has not established the merit of any cognizable objection. Defendant represents it is still determining whether the deponent has relevant information. This is not a valid objection. It must either produce the employee for deposition, object, or move for a protective order. "If a motion under subdivision (a) is granted, the court shall impose a monetary sanction under Chapter 7 (commencing with Section 2023.010) in favor of the party who noticed the deposition and against the deponent or the party with whom the deponent is affiliated, unless the court finds that the one subject to the sanction acted with substantial justification or that other circumstances make the imposition of the sanction unjust." The record does not demonstrate Defendant acted with substantial justification. Plaintiffs, however, did not provide notice of a request for sanctions. For the 9:00 a.m. calendar, all attorneys and parties may appear in Department 302 remotely or in person. Remote hearings will be conducted by videoconference using Zoom. (Dept. 302 Zoom ID 160 409 7690; Passcode 516287.) To appear remotely at the hearing, go to the court's website at sfsuperiorcourt.org under "Online Services," navigate to "Tentative Rulings," and click on the appropriate link, or dial the corresponding phone number. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The text of the email shall include the name and contact information, including email address, of the attorney or party who will appear at the hearing. Counsel for the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the tentative ruling is not contested. The court no longer provides a court reporter in the Law & Motion Department. Parties may retain their own reporter, who may appear in the courtroom or remotely. A retained reporter must be a California certified court reporter (CSR), for only a CSR's transcript may be used in California courts. If a CSR is being retained, include in your email all of the following: their name, CSR and telephone numbers, and their individual work email address. =(302/JMQ)

rulings.Case Title: LIANA FOREST VS. MATTHEW MENASCO ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compel Further Responses To Discovery
rulings.Rulings: Set for Law and Motion/Discovery Calendar on Friday, May 16, 2025, Line 5. DEFENDANT MATTHEW MENASCO's Motion To Compel Further Responses To Discovery. Defendant Matthew Menasco's unopposed Motion to Compel Further Responses to Discovery is GRANTED. Good cause appearing, Plaintiff Liana Forest is ordered to provide responses to Form Interrogatories Nos. 2.3, 2.6, 2.7., 6.3., 6.4., 6.5., 6.6., 8.8., 9.1., 10.1, 12.1., 12.2, 12.4, and 12.6. Responses must be objection free. Good cause appearing, Plaintiff Liana Forest is ordered to provide further responses to Special Interrogatories Nos. 7 & 8. Good cause appearing, Plaintiff Liana Forest is ordered to provide further responses to Requests for Production 1, 2, 5, 6, 7, 8, 12, 13, 22, 24, 25 & 26. Plaintiff has not shown any of her objections have merit. The objections, thus, are overruled. The record does not show that Plaintiff acted with substantial justification with respect to this discovery at issue. Plaintiff and counsel, jointly and severally, shall pay $300 as sanctions, payable to the court by way of the clerk of the court (a copy of this order to accompany payment), payment no later than by June 17, 2025, receipt filed no later than June 23, 2025. DEFENDANT IS ADVISED stacked discovery motions are strongly disfavored. Going forward, stacked motions will be ordered off calendar in favor of individual motions focused each on a single discovery vehicle (e.g., Frogs, SRogs, RFPs, RFAs, deposition notice) addressed to a single, specific party. For the 9:00 a.m. calendar, all attorneys and parties may appear in Department 302 remotely or in person. Remote hearings will be conducted by videoconference using Zoom. (Dept. 302 Zoom ID 160 409 7690; Passcode 516287.) To appear remotely at the hearing, go to the court's website at sfsuperiorcourt.org under "Online Services," navigate to "Tentative Rulings," and click on the appropriate link, or dial the corresponding phone number. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The text of the email shall include the name and contact information, including email address, of the attorney or party who will appear at the hearing. Counsel for the moving party is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the tentative ruling is not contested. The court no longer provides a court reporter in the Law & Motion Department. Parties may retain their own reporter, who may appear in the courtroom or remotely. A retained reporter must be a California certified court reporter (CSR), for only a CSR's transcript may be used in California courts. rulings.Case Title: GEORGE VARKIS VS. BROOKFIELD PROPERTIES MULTIFAMILY, LLC., ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion For Issue, Evidentiary, Terminating, And Monetary Sanctions Against Plaintiff George Varkis; Memorandum Of Points And Authorities; Declaration Of Stephan Choo; And Exhibits In Support Thereof
rulings.Rulings: Set for Law and Motion/Discovery Calendar on Friday, May 16, 2025, Line 9. DEFENDANT BROOKFIELD PROPERTIES MULTIFAMILY, LLC.'s Motion For Issue, Evidentiary, Terminating, And Monetary Sanctions Against Plaintiff George Varkis. Defendant Brookfield Properties Multifamily, LLC's Motion For Issue, Evidentiary, Terminating, And Monetary Sanctions Against Plaintiff George Varkis is GRANTED IN PART AND DENIED IN PART. Good cause appearing and the record demonstrating Plaintiff George Varkis misused discovery by failing to timely comply with the court's discovery orders, Plaintiff George Varkis is to pay to Defendant Brookfield Properties Multifamily, LLC the sum of $2,850 . payable by June 6, 2025, and Plaintiff George Varkis shall pay further sanctions of $1,500 to the court by way of the clerk of the court (a copy of this order to accompany payment), payment no later than by June 17, 2025, receipt filed no later than June 23, 2025. Plaintiff and counsel are admonished to review the California Discovery Act and, going forward, comply with it, including their duties to timely respond to requests and reasonably and in good faith to try to resolve disputes informally. At this time, the additional relief sought by Defendant is denied. Prior to the time set for hearing, Defendant shall lodge by email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org a proposed order setting out the above text verbatim. For the 9:00 a.m. calendar, all attorneys and parties may appear in Department 302 remotely or in person. Remote hearings will be conducted by videoconference using Zoom. rulings.Case Title: LINDA L. PERRY VS. JAMES A. PERRY ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion, And Motion, To Compel James Perry'S Further Responses To Discovery, And For Sanctions
rulings.Rulings: Set for Law and Motion/Discovery Calendar on Friday, May 16, 2025, Line 12. 2 - PLAINTIFF LINDA PERRY's Motion, To Compel James Perry'S Further Responses To Discovery, And For Sanctions. Plaintiff's motion to compel further discovery responses from Deborah Perry is DENIED. First, stacked discovery motions that seek to compel further responses to special interrogatories, form interrogatories, further production, and further responses to the requests for admission are disfavored. Second, plaintiff's separate statements do not comply with CRC 3.1345. CRC 3.1345(c) states that "the separate statement must be full and complete so that no person is required to review any other document in order to determine the full request and the full response." Here, plaintiff's separate statements solely cite defendant's boilerplate objections and exclude the substantive aspects of defendant's responses. (See, e.g., Separate Statement Response to Form Rogs. 2.3, 2.4, 2.5, 2.6, 2.7, 2.8, etc, and Ramirez Decl., Ex. 7; Separate Statement Response to Special Rogs. 2, 3, 7, etc. and Ramirez Decl., Ex. 8; Separate Statement Response to Request for Admission 3, 4, etc. and Ramirez Decl., Ex. 9; Separate Statement Response to Request for Production 2, 3, etc. and Ramirez Decl., Ex. 10.) The separate statements delete the complete responses, are misleading, and do not aid the court. Lastly, defendant provided supplemental responses after this motion was filed that moots the motion and plaintiff's reply does not highlight any existing discovery dispute other than sanctions. (Grosch Decl., pars. 2-4.) The record does not show that either Plaintiff or Defendant James acted with substantial justification in substantial part with respect to this discovery. Plaintiff and Defendant, each, shall pay $125 as sanctions, payable to the court by way of the clerk of the court (a copy of this order to accompany payment), payment no later than by June 17, 2025, receipt filed no later than June 23, 2025. Prior to the time set for hearing, Plaintiff shall lodge by email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org a proposed order setting out the above text verbatim. For the 9:00 a.m. calendar, all attorneys and parties may appear in Department 302 remotely or in person. Remote hearings will be conducted by videoconference using Zoom. (Dept. 302 Zoom ID 160 409 7690; Passcode 516287.) To appear remotely at the hearing, go to the court's website at sfsuperiorcourt.org under "Online Services," navigate to "Tentative Rulings," and click on the appropriate link, or dial the corresponding phone number. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The text of the email shall include the name and contact information, including email address, of the attorney or party who will appear at the hearing. The court no longer provides a court reporter in the Law & Motion Department. Parties may retain their own reporter, who may appear in the courtroom or remotely. A retained reporter must be a California certified court reporter (CSR), for only a CSR's transcript may be used in California courts. If a CSR is being retained, include in your email all of the following: their name, CSR and telephone numbers, and their individual work email address. =(302/JMQ)
